growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron's June quarter showed demand still far ahead of supply, with HBM4 in high-volume shipments and management raising its U.S. investment plan to more than $250 billion through 2035.
growth_picks.analyst_take: HBM and AI memory demand still outweigh cyclicality risk, and the pullback leaves unusually strong upside against a low forward multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Apple expanded its Broadcom relationship through 2031, with SEC and Apple filings showing a large multi-year custom ASIC agreement that reinforces Broadcom's AI and networking silicon franchise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Broadcom combines AI ASIC/networking growth, Apple contract visibility through 2031, and strong FCF with still-meaningful target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Microsoft is 29.6% below its high with Q4 earnings due Jul. 29 after a broad AI-capex selloff; the company is still highly profitable, but the downside case is that AI spending keeps pressuring margins before monetization catches up.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft is a rare overlap mega-cap with strong cloud growth, exceptional margins, a near-term earnings catalyst, and roughly 40% target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Workday's new Developer Agent, Agent-Ready Tools, and Agent Passport are aimed at making enterprise AI agents usable in HR and finance workflows while subscription revenue still grew 13% in the latest quarter.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Workday combines a 40%+ drawdown, strong FCF, cheap forward multiple, and credible enterprise AI-agent product catalysts.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Salesforce reported more than $1 billion of Agentforce ARR and $3.4 billion of combined AI and data ARR, giving the market real monetization proof rather than just AI positioning talk.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce is cheap for its cash generation and has real AI ARR, but Agentforce product-market proof remains contested.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Adobe is down about 37% for the year and recently hit a 52-week low, yet it still posted a revenue and EPS beat with high margins and sizable AI-related recurring revenue, which makes the selloff look disconnected from operating quality.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Adobe looks mispriced on quality, margins, and FCF after a deep drawdown, but AI disruption risk keeps this below strong-buy conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Goldman initiated the stock at Buy after Comfort Systems said data-center work was 56% of Q1 revenue and the company is still seeing strong backlog growth from AI-driven construction demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Comfort Systems has direct AI data-center construction exposure and explosive backlog growth, but valuation leaves little room for disappointment.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista launched a new 1.6T portfolio for AI fabrics and is positioning the network as the backplane for rack-scale AI infrastructure ahead of its August earnings update.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Arista is one of the cleanest AI Ethernet fabric plays, supported by raised guidance and new 1.6T products, though valuation is rich.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Teradyne's robotics business said AI-related demand accounted for nearly 70% of revenue in Q1 2026, and it just showcased production-ready physical AI applications at Automate 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Teradyne has strong AI test demand and a near-term earnings catalyst, but lumpy customer programs and valuation cap conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: AppFolio is 44.8% below its high but only 26% above the low, with Q2 earnings due Jul. 23 after the close; it still has strong revenue growth, a 21.9 forward P/E and PEG below 1, though the risk is that the valuation is no longer cheap.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AppFolio offers a clean software rebound setup with 20% revenue growth and a near-term print after a 45% drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Leidos is roughly 40% below its high and sitting near a 52-week low, with Q2 earnings scheduled for Aug. 4 and valuation still depressed at about 9.8x trailing earnings; the bear case is soft government contract demand, but the setup is classic quality-at-a-discount.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Leidos is a cheap defense/government IT cash generator with large target upside, but growth and leverage are the main checks.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Intuit is still roughly 66% below its 52-week high even after a solid earnings beat, 10.4% revenue growth, and an $8 billion buyback, which is the kind of mismatch between price and fundamentals that can create a high-quality rebound setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Intuit's operating results and buyback are much better than the 60%+ drawdown implies, though AI tax disruption is a real bear case.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Quanta is a direct pick-and-shovel beneficiary of AI data-center and grid buildouts, with a record backlog, raised guidance, and multiple bullish analyst calls supporting the thesis that infrastructure remains one of the best ways to play the current capital-spending cycle.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Quanta is a direct grid and AI infrastructure beneficiary with record backlog and raised guidance, offset by a full multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The Department of War awarded Lockheed a $35.3 billion THAAD interceptor contract on June 24, 2026, and the latest Iran and Ukraine air-defense escalation keeps long-range missile defense procurement at the center of Western rearmament.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed has a major missile-defense catalyst and reasonable forward valuation, but flat growth and leverage keep it from strong-buy status.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: International Seaways explicitly said in current guidance that a prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure would drive meaningful rebalancing and inventory restocking, which is constructive for crude-tanker ton-miles and charter rates.
growth_picks.analyst_take: International Seaways is a cheap tanker cash-flow play with Hormuz-driven rate upside, but the setup is cyclical and geopolitical.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: John C. Malone bought 477,627 shares for about $9.73M on June 23, and Quiver says he has made 9 purchases totaling 15.1M shares (~$135.0M) while CEO Balan Nair and Brendan J. Paddick are also buying, which is a meaningful insider-alignment signal after narrowed losses and a special dividend.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Insider cluster buying and positive FCF support a contrarian telecom value setup, but leverage keeps sizing conservative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Netflix is about 42% below its 52-week high and has Q2 earnings due Jul. 16 after the close; the bear case is engagement and live-TV skepticism, but a clean print could re-rate a market leader that has already been heavily de-rated.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Netflix is a profitable leader with a near-term earnings catalyst and large target upside after a 40%+ drawdown, but engagement concerns are real.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Copart is 45.2% below its high and still under pressure after its February miss, with next earnings estimated for Sep. 3; the bear case is only moderate growth, but the drawdown plus a scheduled reset gives it recovery potential if volumes stabilize.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Copart offers high margins, FCF, a fortress current ratio, and large upside after a deep drawdown if U.S. volumes stabilize.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Western Digital was hit in the recent memory-chip selloff, but it still has a strong earnings backdrop and fresh analyst support, making it a contrarian way to own the storage cycle if AI-driven demand stays intact.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Western Digital is a volatile but credible AI-storage-cycle buy after a memory selloff, supported by analyst target hikes and demand visibility.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: NIO jumped after Goldman upgraded it to Buy and pointed to one of the fastest volume growth trajectories in the EV group, giving the stock a fresh, concrete catalyst beyond the usual China EV noise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: NIO is speculative, but Goldman upgrade support, rapid delivery growth, and a plausible 2026-2027 profit turn justify a contrarian buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Northrop won a June 1, 2026 Navy contract for EA-18G jamming-receiver upgrades and related technical data, a direct beneficiary of the renewed electronic-warfare and integrated air-defense push across NATO and Indo-Pacific air forces.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Northrop offers a defense drawdown recovery setup with low beta, backlog support, and Q2 earnings close enough to catalyze a rerating.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: China added MP Materials to its export-control list on June 22, 2026, and the current rare-earths backdrop still favors non-China supply chains, giving MP a strategic premium as the West tries to de-risk defense and magnet inputs.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is strategically valuable in rare earth supply-chain de-risking, with large upside after a 50% drawdown, but fundamentals are still immature.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Executive Chairman Todd Hannigan bought 300,000 shares for about $1.08M on July 13, a clean open-market C-suite buy in a titanium/minerals name with a sub-$1B market cap and a stock still far below its 52-week high.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IperionX has insider buying, defense-linked titanium optionality, and deep drawdown upside, but it remains early-stage and speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Celcuity has a July 17, 2026 FDA PDUFA date for gedatolisib with Priority Review and Real-Time Oncology Review, making it a classic binary catalyst where the stock can move sharply on the decision.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Celcuity is a high-risk FDA-event buy, with Priority Review days away and major upside if gedatolisib is approved cleanly.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: MannKind has a July 26, 2026 PDUFA date for the FUROSCIX ReadyFlow autoinjector, and management says approval would create a faster IV-equivalent diuretic delivery option, giving the name a near-term event-driven setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MannKind has a near-term de-risked device catalyst, positive FCF, revenue growth, and large target upside after a sizable drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Anthony Noto made a fresh $1 million open-market purchase after a selloff, and the buying fits a pattern of management confidence in a stock that has been pressured more by sentiment than by business quality.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CEO buying is notable, but valuation, high beta, and accounting/credit concerns prevent a fresh buy call.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Nikesh Arora bought about $10 million of stock during a cybersecurity selloff, then the company followed with strong AI-linked cybersecurity results, which is the type of insider signal that can mark a durable bottom in a premium compounder.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Palo Alto remains a high-quality cyber platform with insider support, but current valuation already prices in a lot of AI security upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: GE Vernova sits at the center of the grid, power, and AI-infrastructure rotation, with strong backlog growth and analyst targets clustering near current prices, which suggests investors are still underappreciating the power-demand trade.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GE Vernova is central to the grid and AI power trade, but the stock is close to highs and vulnerable to any AI capex peak narrative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: RTX received a $1.108 billion AIM-9X missile production modification on June 25, 2026, while current NATO/Ukraine coverage highlights urgent Patriot and joint missile-production demand that should keep Raytheon missile lines busy.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has strong missile-demand tailwinds, but the stock is near highs and valuation/upside are less compelling than peers.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: General Dynamics Mission Systems won a June 17, 2026 contract for MK 54 lightweight torpedo components, and the broader undersea-deterrence backdrop remains supportive as naval chokepoint risk rises around the Middle East.
growth_picks.analyst_take: General Dynamics has durable submarine and naval weapons demand, but the stock sits near its high with limited upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: The DOE closed a July 8, 2026 loan of up to $3.26 billion to AEP Texas for roughly 100 transmission projects and 2,800 miles of new or rebuilt lines, directly expanding regulated grid capex tied to U.S. energy resilience.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AEP has a credible regulated grid capex catalyst, but negative FCF, high leverage, and limited upside make it a hold.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Globalharvest Holdings Venture Ltd bought 650,415 shares for about $8.28M on July 6, while directors Bruce C. Taylor and Jay A. Pack had already added 286,410 shares ($3.23M) and 110,719 shares ($1.26M) in June, giving Mission Produce a real accumulation cluster at a roughly $1B market cap.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Mission Produce has a real insider accumulation signal, but weak revenue, low margins, and avocado-price pressure keep it speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: In Q1 2026, Dimensional Fund Advisors added 2,557,079 shares (~$58.9M), Renaissance Technologies added 2,093,755 shares (~$48.2M), Marshall Wace added 1,421,123 shares (~$32.7M), and Alyeska added 1,332,966 shares (~$30.7M), showing broad institutional accumulation into a cash-rich turnaround.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cash-rich balance sheet and institutional accumulation are interesting, but the eBay acquisition push and weak core retail durability make the setup too speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Situational Awareness LP disclosed 10,000,000 shares of T1 Energy worth about $43.9M in Q1 2026, while Renaissance Technologies added 8.29M shares (~$36.4M) and Two Sigma added 7.64M shares (~$33.5M), making this a notable institutional build in a $1.9B solar/manufacturing story with still-elevated short interest.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Institutional accumulation and high short interest create optionality, but losses, leverage, and thin margins make this a watchlist name rather than a buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Dutch Bros short interest fell to 39.72% of float, or 16.09M shares, with only 4.32 days to cover, and the stock was already responding to a raised 2026 outlook, which keeps it near the top of the large-cap squeeze list.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Unit growth and same-store sales are strong, but the valuation already prices in a lot of success despite squeeze potential.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Dollar Tree authorized a new $2.5B repurchase program on July 2 after already repurchasing $500M in June and still having about $700M remaining under the prior authorization, a meaningful capital-return signal at a depressed valuation.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Dollar Tree is executing better after the Family Dollar separation and buyback reset, but limited target upside and tariff risk cap the risk/reward.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv's latest cooling materials say AI servers are pushing cold-plate inlet temperatures to 45C, reinforcing the need for liquid cooling and power systems as new AI racks roll out.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv is a real AI data-center infrastructure winner, but the current multiple leaves little room for a capex-cycle scare.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Sempra Infrastructure helped launch the first U.S. LNG shipment to Asia from Mexico, a route that avoids the Strait of Hormuz and becomes more valuable as Middle East conflict makes Gulf LNG transit riskier.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Sempra has a real LNG security catalyst and solid guidance, but the stock is near highs with weak FCF and limited upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Progressive rebounded 4.4% after a recent slide as the prior session's options tape showed a low put/call ratio and heavy call participation in longer-dated contracts, lining up with improving underwriting trends.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Progressive remains a high-quality insurer with strong underwriting, but the price is too close to consensus fair value to chase.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Citizens cut Upwork on AI disruption concerns after its outlook reset, arguing that faster model progress is hitting more of the freelance marketplace and weakening the core thesis.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Upwork looks statistically cheap, but AI disruption and a reset outlook make the low multiple more likely a value trap than a bargain.
